name: Insomnia
what_it_does: Open-source API design and testing platform from Kong. Insomnia provides a desktop client for designing, debugging, and testing REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket APIs. Key features include environment variables, request chaining, code generation, OpenAPI import/export, and Git Sync for team collaboration. Kong acquired Insomnia in 2019. The Insomnia 8+ CLI (inso) enables running API tests in CI/CD pipelines.
use_cases: Design and prototype APIs using Insomnia's visual request builder before writing application code — export to OpenAPI or generate SDK code
Run API tests in CI/CD pipelines using the inso CLI to automate Insomnia test suites in GitHub Actions or Jenkins
Debug agent API integrations visually — inspect request/response headers, bodies, and timings without writing test code
Manage multiple API environments (dev/staging/prod) using Insomnia's environment variables and environment switching
Collaborate on API collections using Git Sync to version-control API workspaces in team repositories
not_for: Load testing or performance testing — Insomnia is for functional API testing; use k6 or Locust for load testing
Automated browser-based testing — Insomnia tests HTTP APIs only, not browser interactions
Code-first API test automation in CI at scale — Bruno or Newman (Postman CLI) may be better for complex pipeline automation
best_when: You want an open-source Postman alternative for API design and testing with Git-native team collaboration and CLI support for CI/CD integration.
avoid_when: You need a CLI-first, code-native API testing tool or browser automation — Insomnia is primarily a GUI tool with CLI support.

auth.notes: Insomnia is a local desktop app — no auth for the tool itself. Cloud sync (Insomnia Cloud) requires an account. Git Sync uses repository credentials. The tool tests your APIs' auth methods.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Core app is free and open source (Apache 2.0). Local storage and Git Sync are free.
pricing.paid_tiers: Insomnia Cloud storage and team features: paid; Enterprise custom. Insomnia 8+ changed cloud sync to Kong-hosted.
pricing.notes: Major controversy: Insomnia 8.0 removed local storage and required cloud account. Community backlash led to reverting local storage option. Current versions support local-only storage again. Verify privacy settings before storing sensitive API credentials.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local app. Network performance depends on target APIs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Visual HTTP response display with status codes, headers, and body. inso CLI returns exit codes for CI integration. Test assertion failures include clear failure messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP requests are idempotent by nature of the HTTP spec (GET). Insomnia test runs are repeatable. No side effects from the tool itself.
known_agent_gotchas: Insomnia 8.0's forced cloud sync was a major breaking change — verify you're using a version with local storage support (8.4+) to avoid accidentally syncing sensitive credentials to Kong's servers
The inso CLI uses .insomnia workspace export files — workspace must be exported as Git-compatible format first before inso can run tests in CI
Environment variables in Insomnia use {{ _.varName }} syntax — this collides with Nunjucks/Jinja2 syntax that agents may generate; double-check template syntax
Git Sync stores workspaces as .insomnia directory files — these are JSON files that can be large and contain credentials if environment values aren't properly separated
Kong Insomnia's cloud account was required in v8.0-8.3 — many community forks (like Yaak) emerged; verify the fork/version being used
Request chaining (using response values from one request in another) requires Insomnia's response reference syntax — not compatible with other API clients

security_score_components.security_notes: Local-first storage (post-8.4) avoids cloud credential exposure. Kong acquisition raises supply chain trust questions. Electron app with Chromium surface. Apache 2.0 licensed.
